message:
feat(ft-conformance): add fail-closed boundary tests for legacy oracle script output safety

Adds five new test cases that verify the legacy oracle script runner
correctly rejects dangerous or malformed output conditions with
fail-closed behavior. These tests exercise the safety boundaries that
prevent unbounded resource consumption or data corruption from
misbehaving Python oracle scripts.

- Test oversized stdout: infinite write loop triggers stdout size limit
- Test oversized stderr: infinite stderr write triggers stderr size limit
- Test oversized output line: single line exceeding MAX_LEGACY_ORACLE_
  OUTPUT_LINE_BYTES is rejected
- Test non-UTF-8 stdout: raw bytes [0xFF, 0xFE] trigger UTF-8 validation
  failure
- Test empty stdout: script that produces no output is rejected as
  invalid oracle response
- All tests verify python3 availability and skip gracefully if absent

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
